question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint (via a misconfigured HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST env var pointing to an attacker server) cause the pinger to connect to an arbitrary internal host by returning a crafted JSON response with DomainName set to an internal IP, achieving SSRF into the internal network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of URL scheme validation on cfg.mirrorRest to supply a file:// or ftp:// URI, causing the http.Client to read local filesystem paths or trigger unintended protocol handlers, leading to local file disclosure or unexpected behavior?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a cfg.mirrorRest value containing path traversal sequences (e.g., http://attacker.com/../../) that, after strings.TrimRight and strings.TrimSpace processing, result in a URL pointing to an unintended endpoint, bypassing the /api/v1 suffix check?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set cfg.mirrorRest to a URL with embedded credentials (http://user:pass@attacker.com) that are silently forwarded in the HTTP request, leaking credentials to a third-party server via the Referer or Authorization header?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exponential backoff calculation `cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))` with a large mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries value to cause an integer overflow in the bit-shift, resulting in a negative or zero backoff duration and a tight retry loop that exhausts CPU?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server cause the pinger to enter a sustained retry storm by always returning HTTP 429 or 5xx, combined with a large mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries, exhausting goroutine/timer resources and causing service degradation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the backoff timer uses `time.After` (which leaks a timer goroutine until it fires) inside a select with ctx.Done(), causing goroutine accumulation if the context is cancelled rapidly during many concurrent retry loops?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cfg.mirrorRest supply a URL with a fragment (#anchor) that, after strings.TrimRight processing, produces a malformed URL passed to fetchMirrorNodeNetwork(), causing http.NewRequestWithContext to silently strip the fragment and request an unintended path?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a cfg.mirrorRest ending in /api/v1 (matching the strings.HasSuffix check) but with a path injection like http://attacker.com/api/v1%2F..%2Fadmin, causing the constructed URL to resolve to an unintended endpoint on the mirror server?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum retry count upper bound (only checked for < 0) to set mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries to math.MaxInt, causing the retry loop to run indefinitely and block the pinger startup goroutine forever?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST server return a response body of unbounded size (e.g., gigabytes of JSON), causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() to allocate unbounded memory and crash the pinger process via OOM?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a deeply nested or highly recursive JSON structure that causes json.NewDecoder to exhaust stack space or CPU during decoding, resulting in a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a JSON payload with an extremely large Nodes array (millions of entries), causing the `for _, n := range payload.Nodes` loop to allocate a massive network map and exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server inject a nodeEntry with a DomainName containing newline characters or null bytes that, after strings.TrimSpace, are passed to net.JoinHostPort and then used as a gRPC endpoint address, causing connection to an unintended host?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server inject a serviceEndpoint with DomainName set to an internal hostname (e.g., 169.254.169.254 or kubernetes.default.svc), causing the Hiero SDK client to establish gRPC connections to internal cloud metadata services?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server inject a serviceEndpoint with IPAddressV4 containing an IPv6 address (e.g., ::1 or fe80::1), which passes the empty-string check but causes net.JoinHostPort to produce a malformed address string used as a gRPC endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server inject a serviceEndpoint with a DomainName containing URL-encoded characters or percent-encoding that survive strings.TrimSpace and are passed verbatim to net.JoinHostPort, causing DNS resolution of an attacker-controlled hostname?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a response with Content-Type: text/html or application/octet-stream (no Content-Type validation is performed), causing json.NewDecoder to attempt to parse non-JSON data and potentially trigger unexpected decoder behavior?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return HTTP 301/302 redirects pointing to internal services, exploiting the default http.Client redirect-following behavior to achieve SSRF against internal endpoints not directly reachable from the pinger's configured URL?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a response with Transfer-Encoding: chunked and a malformed chunk size, causing the Go HTTP client to hang indefinitely reading the body if the mirrorNodeClientTimeout is not enforced at the body-read level?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a valid HTTP 200 response but with a body that streams data extremely slowly (1 byte/second), bypassing the connection timeout since the timeout only applies to the full round-trip, causing the pinger to hang indefinitely on json.Decode?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server inject a nodeEntry with NodeAccountID set to a malformed string that causes hiero.AccountIDFromString to panic (rather than return an error), crashing the pinger process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry items have empty NodeAccountID, causing the network map to remain empty and triggering the 'no usable service_endpoints found' error, effectively preventing the pinger from ever initializing and causing a permanent denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a nodesEnvelope where all serviceEndpoint entries have Port == 0, causing all endpoints to be skipped and the network map to be empty, triggering a non-retryable error that permanently blocks pinger startup?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: '] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST server return a nodesEnvelope where all serviceEndpoint entries have both DomainName and IPAddressV4 as empty strings, causing all endpoints to be filtered out and the pinger to fail initialization with a non-retryable error?
